description: Action spectra for biological response channels. Pinned to specific published references. Each entry maps wavelength (nm) → relative weighting (0-1).

references.cie_erythemal: CIE 174:2006 / McKinlay-Diffey 1987 — erythemal action spectrum (skin reddening / DNA damage proxy)
references.cie_vitamin_d: CIE 174:2006 — vitamin D3 production action spectrum (7-DHC photoisomerization, MacLaughlin 1982)
references.cie_melanopic: CIE S 026:2018 — melanopic photopic / ipRGC sensitivity, peaks 480nm
references.opn3_uva: Regazzetti et al. 2018 (J Invest Dermatol) — OPN3 UVA response in dermal fibroblasts
references.opn5_violet: Buhr et al. 2019 — OPN5 short-wavelength sensitivity, dual peak ~380nm + ~471nm
references.cco_pbm: Karu 1999 / Hamblin 2018 — cytochrome-c oxidase action spectrum, peaks at 620, 670, 760, 830nm
references.no_release: Liu et al. 2014 (J Invest Dermatol) / Oplander 2009 — UVA-induced cutaneous NO release (photolabile RSNO + nitrite decomposition)

vitamin_d.label: Vitamin D synthesis
vitamin_d.notes: Photochemical (not receptor-mediated). 7-DHC → previtD3. Saturates ~1 MED, reverses to inert tachysterol/lumisterol with continued exposure.

pomc.label: POMC / melanocortin
pomc.notes: OPN5 → POMC → α-MSH/ACTH/β-endorphin pathway. Erythemal action spectrum used as proxy.

no_cv.label: NO / cardiovascular
no_cv.notes: Photolabile cutaneous nitrite + S-nitrosothiol decomposition releases NO. NOS-independent. Peak ~330-360nm.

violet_eye.label: Violet / outdoor-eye signal
violet_eye.notes: OPN5 in skin (melanogenesis kickoff) + retinal OPN5 (axial-length regulation, Tsubota/Torii). Broader than myopia framing.

circadian.label: Circadian (melanopic)
circadian.notes: Melanopsin ipRGC → SCN. CIE S 026:2018 standard. Effective even at moderate intensities (40 lux + 80min → measurable CAR boost).

nir_solar.label: Mitochondrial (NIR-solar broadband)
nir_solar.notes: Wunsch optical tissue window. Solar IR-A penetrates head-to-knee (Jeffery+Fosbury 2025). Indoor windows with Low-E glass kill this channel.

pbm_red.label: PBM red (narrowband)
pbm_red.notes: Narrowband artificial only — therapy panel input. Separate from NIR-solar because tissue depth + receptor coverage differ.

pbm_nir.label: PBM near-IR (narrowband)
pbm_nir.notes: Narrowband artificial only. 810/850nm typical panels. Deeper penetration than red.

d1_sleep_darkness.label: Sleep-time darkness
d1_sleep_darkness.target: <0.3 lux at eye during sleep window
d1_sleep_darkness.rationale: Reiter 2020 — melatonin amplitude attenuated even at sub-lux ALAN exposure. Wunsch — full darkness prerequisites for full nocturnal endocrine cascade.

d2_daylight_deficit.label: Daytime indoor-light deficit
d2_daylight_deficit.rationale: Ott 1973 — malillumination; Hattar/Huberman — minimum effective AM dose for SCN entrainment.

d3_junk_light_contamination.label: Junk-light contamination
d3_junk_light_contamination.rationale: Wunsch — spectrum-truncation stress; IEEE PAR1789 — flicker risk thresholds; Reiter — blue-suppression of pre-sleep melatonin.

_action_spectra_full: Full per-nm action spectra arrays embedded into js/sun-spectrum.js to keep this JSON small. The references above are the source-of-truth citations; the numeric tables are versioned alongside the model.
